meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders Guide: Portfolio Building to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Learn how to leverage portfolio building to generate SaaS revenue using Antigravity and Claude Code in this guide.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, treat your portfolio as a series of micro-SaaS experiments. By building modular, high-value utilities that solve specific niche pain points, you validate market demand, build authority, and create a verifiable track record that attracts investors and paying customers simultaneously.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of founders. You have the technical stack, the ambition, and the hunger, yet you remain paralyzed by the search for the 'billion-dollar idea.' This stagnation is a death sentence for your momentum. The reality is that the best ideas aren't found; they are engineered through iterative, market-facing development.

Portfolio building is your antidote. It shifts your focus from abstract conceptualization to tangible execution. By shipping small, functional assets, you transform your development process into a laboratory for monetization. Stop dreaming of the unicorn and start building the ecosystem that makes one inevitable.

steps.title: Identify a Micro-Problem
steps.description: Analyze your own workflow to find a recurring friction point. If it takes you 15 minutes to do something that should take 15 seconds, that is your MVP.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my recent GitHub commit history and developer workflow notes to identify three distinct, repetitive tasks that could be automated into a micro-SaaS tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze my project directory and suggest 3 micro-SaaS opportunities based on existing code patterns'

steps.title: Architect the Minimum Viable Asset
steps.description: Design a modular component that solves the identified problem. Keep scope tight to ensure shipping within 48 hours.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, modular architecture for a Next.js application that solves [Problem]. Focus on high-performance API routes and minimal database overhead.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold a Next.js project with Prisma and Tailwind that implements the core logic for [Problem]'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to rapidly build the engine. Focus on the 'happy path' first to ensure the core value proposition is functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the business logic for the core feature, ensuring robust error handling and clean separation of concerns for future scaling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement the core service layer for [Feature] with unit tests'

steps.title: Add Monetization Hooks
steps.description: Don't build for free. Integrate Stripe or a simple subscription gate early to test if users are willing to pay for the solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Stripe Checkout and a basic usage-based billing tier into the existing codebase, ensuring secure session handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'integrate Stripe checkout flow with a basic subscription model for the user dashboard'

steps.title: Ship and Iterate
steps.description: Deploy to a production environment. Use feedback loops to pivot or double down based on real user engagement data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist and a post-launch analytics tracking plan to monitor user conversion and feature engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a deployment script for Vercel and set up post-launch monitoring'

pro_tips: Build 'utility-first' rather than 'feature-first'; solve a specific, painful problem for a niche audience to guarantee early traction.
Use Claude Code to write your documentation as you code; a project without documentation is just a liability, not an asset.
Never hardcode pricing; build a configuration layer that allows you to toggle tiers and pricing models without redeploying.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Value' (TTFV) over feature complexity; if the user doesn't get value in under 60 seconds, they will churn.
Treat your GitHub profile as a sales landing page; clean READMEs with clear ROI statements are more valuable than stars.
content.next_steps: Once your first micro-SaaS is live, spend 72 hours on cold outreach to your target niche. Use the feedback to decide whether to scale this project or use the learnings to build your next, more refined tool.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework, the tools, and the strategy. The only thing missing is your execution. Don't let this knowledge sit idle; ship your first project and start building your reputation as a founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
